task_completion.description: Verify the agent has successfully logged into the OpenSIS system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or portal is visible in a web browser, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Discipline Offense Codes configuration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS navigation menu shows Discipline setup or configuration selected, and the Offense Codes management page is displayed.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent has opened the form or interface to add a new offense code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form, modal, or new input row is visible specifically for adding a new Discipline Offense Code.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct title and sort order for the new offense code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Cyberbullying' is typed into the offense title/name field, and the number '10' is entered into the sort order field.

task_completion.description: Verify the new offense code is successfully saved and listed in the system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main list of Discipline Offense Codes now displays 'Cyberbullying' with a sort order of '10', or a success message is visible confirming the record was added.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is interacting with the OpenSIS web interface in a browser, not using terminal commands (like curl) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not directly manipulate the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing MySQL prompts or direct SQL INSERT commands into the 'discipline_field_usage' table.

integrity.description: Verify the results are in the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and final results are shown within the standard OpenSIS web interface, not mocked up in a text editor or by modifying the local HTML via browser developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected title for the new offense code is exactly 'Cyberbullying'. The agent must add this through the OpenSIS web interface. The underlying database table being updated is 'discipline_field_usage', but verification should rely entirely on the OpenSIS GUI showing the new offense code successfully added with a sort order of '10'.
